1	// Design tokens — single source of truth for the Marketify visual language.
2	// Mirrors docs/design.md §1.2 (palette), §1.5 (corners/borders/shadow), and §4 (16px gutter, 12px vertical rhythm).
3	// Values are `as const` so downstream code gets literal types, not widened string/number.
4	
5	export const colors = {
6	  primary: '#EC4899',
7	  primarySoft: '#FDF2F8',
8	  primaryDeep: '#831843',
9	
10	  cta: '#F97316',
11	  ctaDeep: '#9A3412',
12	
13	  ink: '#0F172A',
14	  ink70: '#475569',
15	  ink40: '#94A3B8',
16	
17	  surface: '#FFFFFF',
18	  canvas: '#FFF9F2',
19	  hairline: '#E5E7EB',
20	
21	  success: '#16A34A',
22	  successSoft: '#DCFCE7',
23	  danger: '#DC2626',
24	  dangerSoft: '#FEE2E2',
25	  warning: '#F59E0B',
26	  warningSoft: '#FEF3C7',
27	  cancelled: '#6B7280',
28	  cancelledSoft: '#F3F4F6',
29	
30	  // Shadow color is always ink per spec §1.2 row "Shadow" — kept as a named alias for palette completeness.
31	  shadow: '#0F172A',
32	} as const;
33	
34	export const spacing = {
35	  xs: 4,
36	  sm: 8,
37	  md: 12,
38	  base: 16,
39	  lg: 20,
40	  xl: 24,
41	  xxl: 32,
42	  xxxl: 48,
43	} as const;
44	
45	export const radii = {
46	  card: 12,
47	  button: 10,
48	  input: 10,
49	  image: 8,
50	  pill: 999,
51	} as const;
52	
53	// Neubrutalist hard shadow — solid ink offset (3,3), opacity 1, radius 0.
54	// RN android uses `elevation`; the hard-shadow look is iOS-primary, android gets a matching elevation hint.
55	export const shadows = {
56	  hard: {
57	    shadowColor: colors.ink,
58	    shadowOffset: { width: 3, height: 3 },
59	    shadowOpacity: 1,
60	    shadowRadius: 0,
61	    elevation: 4,
62	  },
63	  none: {
64	    shadowColor: 'transparent',
65	    shadowOffset: { width: 0, height: 0 },
66	    shadowOpacity: 0,
67	    shadowRadius: 0,

1	// Typography — typed text-style presets for the Marketify design system.
2	// Mirrors docs/design.md §1.3 (font pairing table + line-height rules).
3	//
4	// Font substitution: docs/design.md names Clash Display (headings) and Satoshi (body).
5	// Both are Fontshare-hosted and do not ship with Expo Google Fonts; we use the explicit
6	// fallbacks listed in §1.3 — Outfit for display/headings, Rubik for body/caption — plus
7	// JetBrains Mono for monospace. Spec-sanctioned per the same section.
8	//
9	// `fontFamily` values are the string keys registered with `expo-font.useFonts` in
10	// `app/_layout.tsx`. They MUST match the constant names imported from
11	// `@expo-google-fonts/*` so RN can resolve the loaded assets by name.
12	
13	import {
14	  Outfit_600SemiBold,
15	  Outfit_700Bold,
16	} from '@expo-google-fonts/outfit';
17	import {
18	  Rubik_500Medium,
19	  Rubik_700Bold,
20	} from '@expo-google-fonts/rubik';
21	import { JetBrainsMono_500Medium } from '@expo-google-fonts/jetbrains-mono';
22	import type { TextStyle } from 'react-native';
23	
24	export const fontFamilies = {
25	  displayBold: 'Outfit_700Bold',
26	  displaySemi: 'Outfit_600SemiBold',
27	  bodyMedium: 'Rubik_500Medium',
28	  bodyBold: 'Rubik_700Bold',
29	  mono: 'JetBrainsMono_500Medium',
30	} as const;
31	
32	// Map consumed by `useFonts` — values are the numeric asset handles exported by
33	// @expo-google-fonts/*, keys are the runtime font-family names.
34	export const fontAssets = {
35	  [fontFamilies.displayBold]: Outfit_700Bold,
36	  [fontFamilies.displaySemi]: Outfit_600SemiBold,
37	  [fontFamilies.bodyMedium]: Rubik_500Medium,
38	  [fontFamilies.bodyBold]: Rubik_700Bold,
39	  [fontFamilies.mono]: JetBrainsMono_500Medium,
40	} as const;
41	
42	// Line heights per §1.3: 1.15 for display, ~1.55 for body. Caption + mono tighten
43	// to 1.4 for dense data rows.
44	export const textStyles = {
45	  display: {
46	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.displayBold,
47	    fontSize: 28,
48	    lineHeight: 32,
49	  },
50	  h1: {
51	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.displaySemi,
52	    fontSize: 22,
53	    lineHeight: 25,
54	  },
55	  h2: {
56	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.displaySemi,
57	    fontSize: 20,
58	    lineHeight: 23,
59	  },
60	  body: {
61	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.bodyMedium,
62	    fontSize: 15,
63	    lineHeight: 23,
64	  },
65	  caption: {
66	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.bodyMedium,
67	    fontSize: 13,
68	    lineHeight: 18,
69	  },
70	  mono: {
71	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.mono,
72	    fontSize: 14,
73	    lineHeight: 20,
74	  },
75	  // Micro (pills + chip labels) per §1.3 — Rubik_700Bold stands in for Satoshi 700,
76	  // 0.08em tracking at 11pt = 0.88pt (RN letterSpacing is absolute points).
77	  micro: {
78	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.bodyBold,
79	    fontSize: 11,
80	    lineHeight: 14,
81	    letterSpacing: 0.88,
82	    textTransform: 'uppercase',
83	  },
84	} as const satisfies Record<string, TextStyle>;
85	
86	export type TextStyleToken = keyof typeof textStyles;
87	export type FontFamilyToken = keyof typeof fontFamilies;

input.prompt:
Review US-035 "Creator profile screen — handles + metrics + add/unlink" before I commit it.

## Acceptance criteria (from scripts/ralph/prd.json story US-035)
- app/(creator)/profile.tsx queries creator_profiles + social_links
- Renders TikTok + IG sections with monospace handles, follower counts, avg views, last_refreshed_at relative time
- Add Handle / Unlink Handle actions wired to a small edge function `manage-social-link` (created as part of this story)
- 'Stale' chip appears when metrics_stale_at is non-null
- Mobile-mcp verification (not your concern — I'll run it)

## Authoritative docs (context for ambiguities)
- docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 (schema for creator_profiles, social_links, metric_snapshots), §5 (edge function conventions), §16 (cron jobs — `mark-metrics-stale` hourly flips metric_snapshots.status='stale' after 24h)
- docs/design.md §1.2 (palette incl. warning + warningSoft for the stale chip), §1.3 (JetBrains Mono for handles), §1.5 (2px ink border + hard shadow on cards/chips), §3.3 (bottom sheet)
- scripts/ralph/CLAUDE.md (conventions — spec wins over AC when they disagree; "NoA" column means spec gap)

## Files in scope
- NEW supabase/functions/manage-social-link/index.ts (already deployed; version 1, slug manage-social-link)
- NEW supabase/migrations/20260417230000_us_035_manage_social_link_fn.sql (already applied; advisors clean except a pre-existing INFO finding on public.events unrelated to this story)
- NEW src/lib/time.ts (relative-time formatter, will be reused by US-036/US-043 etc.)
- REWRITE app/(creator)/profile.tsx (was a TabPlaceholder + sign-out; now loads real data)

## Known spec gap (documented in the edge function header + in the profile screen header comment)
The AC names a `metrics_stale_at` column on creator_profiles. That column does NOT exist (see us_004 + the us_011 cron job). I derived the stale chip from `metric_snapshots WHERE is_latest = true AND status = 'stale'` — that is the actual signal the `mark-metrics-stale` cron produces per tech-architecture §16. Confirm this is the correct interpretation before I move on.

## Specific things to sanity-check
1. **Edge function auth path** — `manage-social-link/index.ts` verifies a Marketify HS256 JWT via `_shared/jwt.ts` and requires role='creator'. Deployed with verify_jwt=false at the gateway. Contract is all in the file header.
2. **RPC contract vs. edge function mapping** — `public.manage_social_link` RPC returns jsonb. On add success: {social_link_id}. On unlink success: {ok: true}. On conflict: {error: ALREADY_LINKED|HANDLE_TAKEN|LINK_NOT_FOUND|INVALID_REQUEST}. The unique-violation branch reads `GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS v_constraint` and maps by index name (`social_links_user_platform_uniq` → ALREADY_LINKED, `social_links_platform_handle_uniq` → HANDLE_TAKEN). Confirm these index names are correct per us_004 — I looked at the live schema via MCP list_tables but want a second pair of eyes.
3. **Unlink semantics** — per docs/tech-architecture.md, "unlinked" = set status='unlinked' (NOT delete). The partial unique indexes are scoped to `status <> 'unlinked'`, so unlinking a handle frees the (user, platform) and (platform, handle) slots for re-use. Confirm.
4. **Handle normalization** — edge function strips leading `@` + trims, then validates `^[a-zA-Z0-9_.]{1,30}$` (matches the US-034 signup regex). RPC trusts the caller (accepts citext). Client (profile.tsx) does its own HANDLE_RE check before invoke.
5. **Client 409 mapping** — profile.tsx distinguishes ALREADY_LINKED (same user already has a linked handle on this platform) from HANDLE_TAKEN (another user owns this handle). Both map to different toasts. Confirm my toast copy is clear enough.
6. **Staleness query** — `supabase.from('metric_snapshots').select('social_link_id, status').eq('is_latest', true).eq('status', 'stale')` — RLS will filter to the current user's rows. Is there a concern with reading metric_snapshots directly from the client? Check RLS policies in supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql.
7. **BottomSheet re-use** — I render the sheet once and drive it from `sheetPlatform: Platform | null`. The inner TextInput state resets via useEffect([platform]). Confirm no stale state leak when the user opens TikTok sheet, dismisses, then opens Instagram sheet.
8. **Sign-out wiring** — the previous placeholder had a sign-out button; I preserved it (ButtonSecondary with testID='creator-signout') under a sectioned layout. US-037 will lift the pattern into the lister profile.

## What you don't need to check
- Mobile-mcp verification (I run this after your review)
- Pull-to-refresh (that's US-036, explicitly out of scope)
- Apify scrape dispatch on add (out of scope per the story AC wording; US-036 handles metric refresh)

Report high-priority issues only — medium-or-higher severity. Low-confidence or nit-level stuff I'd rather skip at this point in the loop. Reference files by path:line.

content.text:
The error shape decoder in `profile.tsx` matches the established pattern used in `creator.tsx`. Now I have all I need for the review. Let me compile my findings:

---

Reviewing `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(creator)/profile.tsx`, `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/functions/manage-social-link/index.ts`, `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417230000_us_035_manage_social_link_fn.sql`, and `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/lib/time.ts` against the US-035 AC, the schema in `us_004`/`us_007`, and the RLS policies in `us_009`.

---

## Critical

### 1. Stale chip is permanently broken — RLS blocks the staleness query (Confidence: 100)

`/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(creator)/profile.tsx` lines 72-76:

```ts
supabase
  .from('metric_snapshots')
  .select('social_link_id, status')
  .eq('is_latest', true)
  .eq('status', 'stale'),
```

The RLS policy in `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql` lines 123-132 gates **all client-accessible reads** on `metric_snapshots` with `is_latest AND status = 'fresh'`. There is no second policy that relaxes this for stale rows. The comment on the policy says explicitly: "Only is_latest + status='fresh' rows are readable; history is service-role only."

The consequence: `snapsRes.data` will always be an empty array regardless of how many stale snapshots exist. `staleLinkIds` is always an empty `Set`. The `StaleChip` never renders. The feature is completely non-functional without any runtime error to warn you.

The fix is not in the client query — that query is correctly expressing the intent. The fix must be in the RLS layer. The policy needs a second variant for the creator self-read path that allows `is_latest AND (status = 'fresh' OR status = 'stale')`. Adding a dedicated service-role function that returns staleness per `social_link_id` for the caller's own links (like a thin RPC) is another option and keeps the RLS policy tight. Either way, the client query cannot be made to work as written under the current policy.

---

## Important

### 2. Spec gap interpretation is correct, but confirm before committing (Confidence: 85)

You asked for confirmation: yes, deriving the stale chip from `metric_snapshots WHERE is_latest = true AND status = 'stale'` is the correct interpretation of the cron's intent. `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417163000_us_011_cron_jobs.sql` lines 50-57 shows the cron flips `status` from `'fresh'` to `'stale'` on the `is_latest` row directly — that is the exact column your query targets. The AC's reference to `metrics_stale_at` is a spec gap; the column does not exist on `creator_profiles` in `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417090000_us_004_identity_tables.sql`. Your interpretation is right, the query target is right, only the RLS wall (issue #1 above) prevents it from working.

### 3. Responses to your specific sanity-check items

**Item 2 — Index names.** Confirmed correct. `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417090000_us_004_identity_tables.sql` lines 75-78 define exactly `social_links_user_platform_uniq` and `social_links_platform_handle_uniq` with the `status <> 'unlinked'` predicates. The `GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS` branch in the RPC maps them correctly.

**Item 3 — Unlink semantics.** Confirmed. The RPC at `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417230000_us_035_manage_social_link_fn.sql` lines 52-57 does `SET status = 'unlinked'` with a `status <> 'unlinked'` guard in the WHERE clause. The partial unique indexes scope `status <> 'unlinked'`, so unlinking does release both uniqueness slots for re-use. Correct.

These are unambiguous and consistent with the pattern in `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(auth)/signup/creator.tsx` line 149. No concern here.

**Item 6 — Staleness query RLS.** This is issue #1 above — confirmed problematic. The two RLS policies on `metric_snapshots` (lines 123-145 of `us_009`) both require `status = 'fresh'`. Stale rows are not readable from the client with the current policy set.

Since `platform` changes value (TikTok → null → Instagram), the reset fires on each open. If a user types in the TikTok sheet, dismisses (`platform → null`, `value → ''`), then opens Instagram (`platform → 'instagram'`, `value → ''` again), no stale text survives. Confirmed clean.

Review US-035 "Creator profile screen — handles + metrics + add/unlink" before I commit it.

## Acceptance criteria (from scripts/ralph/prd.json story US-035)
- app/(creator)/profile.tsx queries creator_profiles + social_links
- Renders TikTok + IG sections with monospace handles, follower counts, avg views, last_refreshed_at relative time
- Add Handle / Unlink Handle actions wired to a small edge function `manage-social-link` (created as part of this story)
- 'Stale' chip appears when metrics_stale_at is non-null
- Mobile-mcp verification (not your concern — I'll run it)

## Authoritative docs (context for ambiguities)
- docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 (schema for creator_profiles, social_links, metric_snapshots), §5 (edge function conventions), §16 (cron jobs — `mark-metrics-stale` hourly flips metric_snapshots.status='stale' after 24h)
- docs/design.md §1.2 (palette incl. warning + warningSoft for the stale chip), §1.3 (JetBrains Mono for handles), §1.5 (2px ink border + hard shadow on cards/chips), §3.3 (bottom sheet)
- scripts/ralph/CLAUDE.md (conventions — spec wins over AC when they disagree; "NoA" column means spec gap)

## Files in scope
- NEW supabase/functions/manage-social-link/index.ts (already deployed; version 1, slug manage-social-link)
- NEW supabase/migrations/20260417230000_us_035_manage_social_link_fn.sql (already applied; advisors clean except a pre-existing INFO finding on public.events unrelated to this story)
- NEW src/lib/time.ts (relative-time formatter, will be reused by US-036/US-043 etc.)
- REWRITE app/(creator)/profile.tsx (was a TabPlaceholder + sign-out; now loads real data)

## Known spec gap (documented in the edge function header + in the profile screen header comment)
The AC names a `metrics_stale_at` column on creator_profiles. That column does NOT exist (see us_004 + the us_011 cron job). I derived the stale chip from `metric_snapshots WHERE is_latest = true AND status = 'stale'` — that is the actual signal the `mark-metrics-stale` cron produces per tech-architecture §16. Confirm this is the correct interpretation before I move on.

## Specific things to sanity-check
1. **Edge function auth path** — `manage-social-link/index.ts` verifies a Marketify HS256 JWT via `_shared/jwt.ts` and requires role='creator'. Deployed with verify_jwt=false at the gateway. Contract is all in the file header.
2. **RPC contract vs. edge function mapping** — `public.manage_social_link` RPC returns jsonb. On add success: {social_link_id}. On unlink success: {ok: true}. On conflict: {error: ALREADY_LINKED|HANDLE_TAKEN|LINK_NOT_FOUND|INVALID_REQUEST}. The unique-violation branch reads `GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS v_constraint` and maps by index name (`social_links_user_platform_uniq` → ALREADY_LINKED, `social_links_platform_handle_uniq` → HANDLE_TAKEN). Confirm these index names are correct per us_004 — I looked at the live schema via MCP list_tables but want a second pair of eyes.
3. **Unlink semantics** — per docs/tech-architecture.md, "unlinked" = set status='unlinked' (NOT delete). The partial unique indexes are scoped to `status <> 'unlinked'`, so unlinking a handle frees the (user, platform) and (platform, handle) slots for re-use. Confirm.
4. **Handle normalization** — edge function strips leading `@` + trims, then validates `^[a-zA-Z0-9_.]{1,30}$` (matches the US-034 signup regex). RPC trusts the caller (accepts citext). Client (profile.tsx) does its own HANDLE_RE check before invoke.
5. **Client 409 mapping** — profile.tsx distinguishes ALREADY_LINKED (same user already has a linked handle on this platform) from HANDLE_TAKEN (another user owns this handle). Both map to different toasts. Confirm my toast copy is clear enough.
6. **Staleness query** — `supabase.from('metric_snapshots').select('social_link_id, status').eq('is_latest', true).eq('status', 'stale')` — RLS will filter to the current user's rows. Is there a concern with reading metric_snapshots directly from the client? Check RLS policies in supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql.
7. **BottomSheet re-use** — I render the sheet once and drive it from `sheetPlatform: Platform | null`. The inner TextInput state resets via useEffect([platform]). Confirm no stale state leak when the user opens TikTok sheet, dismisses, then opens Instagram sheet.
8. **Sign-out wiring** — the previous placeholder had a sign-out button; I preserved it (ButtonSecondary with testID='creator-signout') under a sectioned layout. US-037 will lift the pattern into the lister profile.

## What you don't need to check
- Mobile-mcp verification (I run this after your review)
- Pull-to-refresh (that's US-036, explicitly out of scope)
- Apify scrape dispatch on add (out of scope per the story AC wording; US-036 handles metric refresh)

Report high-priority issues only — medium-or-higher severity. Low-confidence or nit-level stuff I'd rather skip at this point in the loop. Reference files by path:line.

The error shape decoder in `profile.tsx` matches the established pattern used in `creator.tsx`. Now I have all I need for the review. Let me compile my findings:

---

Reviewing `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(creator)/profile.tsx`, `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/functions/manage-social-link/index.ts`, `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417230000_us_035_manage_social_link_fn.sql`, and `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/lib/time.ts` against the US-035 AC, the schema in `us_004`/`us_007`, and the RLS policies in `us_009`.

## Critical

### 1. Stale chip is permanently broken — RLS blocks the staleness query (Confidence: 100)

`/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(creator)/profile.tsx` lines 72-76:

```ts
supabase
  .from('metric_snapshots')
  .select('social_link_id, status')
  .eq('is_latest', true)
  .eq('status', 'stale'),
```

The RLS policy in `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql` lines 123-132 gates **all client-accessible reads** on `metric_snapshots` with `is_latest AND status = 'fresh'`. There is no second policy that relaxes this for stale rows. The comment on the policy says explicitly: "Only is_latest + status='fresh' rows are readable; history is service-role only."

The consequence: `snapsRes.data` will always be an empty array regardless of how many stale snapshots exist. `staleLinkIds` is always an empty `Set`. The `StaleChip` never renders. The feature is completely non-functional without any runtime error to warn you.

The fix is not in the client query — that query is correctly expressing the intent. The fix must be in the RLS layer. The policy needs a second variant for the creator self-read path that allows `is_latest AND (status = 'fresh' OR status = 'stale')`. Adding a dedicated service-role function that returns staleness per `social_link_id` for the caller's own links (like a thin RPC) is another option and keeps the RLS policy tight. Either way, the client query cannot be made to work as written under the current policy.

---

You asked for confirmation: yes, deriving the stale chip from `metric_snapshots WHERE is_latest = true AND status = 'stale'` is the correct interpretation of the cron's intent. `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417163000_us_011_cron_jobs.sql` lines 50-57 shows the cron flips `status` from `'fresh'` to `'stale'` on the `is_latest` row directly — that is the exact column your query targets. The AC's reference to `metrics_stale_at` is a spec gap; the column does not exist on `creator_profiles` in `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417090000_us_004_identity_tables.sql`. Your interpretation is right, the query target is right, only the RLS wall (issue #1 above) prevents it from working.

**Item 5 — Client 409 mapping.** The toast copy is clear. "You already have a TikTok handle linked" (ALREADY_LINKED) is correctly scoped to the current user. "That handle is already linked to another account" (HANDLE_TAKEN) is correctly scoped to a different user. input.name: us_035_metric_snapshots_self_read_stale
input.query:
-- US-035 — widen metric_snapshots_self_read so creators can see their own
-- is_latest row when it has been flipped to status='stale' by the hourly
-- `mark-metrics-stale` cron (us_011).
--
-- Before: policy required status='fresh'. Result: the Profile screen's
-- stale-chip query returned zero rows even when snapshots were legitimately
-- stale, because RLS silently filtered them out. There was no way for a
-- creator to learn their metrics had aged past 24h without the server
-- computing it for them.
--
-- After: creators can select their own is_latest snapshot regardless of
-- fresh vs. stale. 'refreshing' and 'failed' rows stay service-role only —
-- those represent in-flight or error states the client learns about via
-- edge-function responses (metrics-refresh / apify-webhook), not direct
-- table reads. The lister-read policy stays unchanged; listers only ever
-- need a successful eligibility snapshot for their applicant reviews.

drop policy if exists metric_snapshots_self_read on public.metric_snapshots;
create policy metric_snapshots_self_read on public.metric_snapshots for select
  using (
    is_latest and status in ('fresh', 'stale')
    and exists (
      select 1 from public.social_links sl
      where sl.id = public.metric_snapshots.social_link_id
        and sl.user_id = public.current_user_id()
    )
  );

comment on policy metric_snapshots_self_read on public.metric_snapshots is
  'Creator self-read of own is_latest snapshots (fresh + stale). Widened in us_035 so the Profile screen can surface the Stale chip — the hourly mark-metrics-stale cron flips status from fresh to stale in place on the same is_latest row.';
